content: Measuring appropriate antibiotic use: (1) Outpatient metrics - % of URI visits with antibiotics (target <35%), % of strep pharyngitis with testing before prescribing (target >90%); (2) Inpatient metrics - days of therapy per 1,000 patient-days, % compliance with surgical prophylaxis guidelines (stop within 24 hours); (3) Resistance surveillance - CDC's AR Isolate Bank tracks resistance patterns; (4) Peer comparison - facility-level prescribing compared to regional/national benchmarks. High-performing systems achieve: <25% antibiotic prescribing for viral URIs, >95% guideline-concordant surgical prophylaxis, <400 days of therapy/1,000 patient-days. Regional variation persists: some US states have 2x higher prescribing rates than others despite similar disease burden, suggesting practice pattern rather than patient need drives variation.
